---
title: "Agent Engineering in BI"
subtitle: "Building AI That Actually Works For Your Data Team"
author: "Scott Bell"
date: "2026-04-08"
draft: true
format:
  myyearindata-slides-revealjs:
    footer: "myyearindata.com"
    logo: "../../logos/RapiData-White.png"
---


## {.title-slide}

::: v-center
# Agent Engineering in BI

### The concepts you need to [build AI that actually works]{.highlight} for your data team

::: {.subtext .doodle-text}
SQLBits 2026 — Scott Bell — @fusionet24
:::
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Let's be honest. {.center}

::: r-fit-text
You've used AI.
:::

. . .

::: {.subtext}
It was... [fine]{.doodle-underline}.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## The gap isn't the technology. {.center}

::: v-center

The gap is understanding [how agents actually work]{.doodle-circle .highlight} under the hood.

. . .

::: subtext
Once you understand the moving parts, everything clicks.
:::

:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What we'll cover {.smaller}

::: card-grid
::: card
### 1. Context Engineering
What a "prompt" actually is — and why it's 10x bigger than you think
:::

::: card
### 2. The ReAct Loop
How agents think, act, and learn from observations
:::

::: card
### 3. Tool Calling
How agents discover and use tools — and what that means for BI
:::

::: card
### 4. Agent Skills
Bundling tools with domain knowledge for real capability
:::

::: card
### 5. Hooks & Security
Intercepting agent actions before things go wrong
:::

::: card
### 6. Agent Harnesses
The scaffolding that keeps everything safe and manageable
:::
:::

. . .

::: {.doodle-text}
Plus: an Agent Maturity Model to figure out where you are and where to go next.
:::


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## {.section-slide}

# Part 1

Context Engineering & The Prompt

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What do you think a prompt is? {.center}

::: comparison
::: box
### What most people think

A text box where you type instructions to an LLM.

*"Write me a SQL query for..."*
:::

[→]{.arrow}

::: {.box .active}
### What it actually is

The **entire assembled context** the model sees — system instructions, memory, tool schemas, conversation history, and yes, your message.
:::
:::

::: {.subtext .doodle-text}
Your message? Maybe 5% of the actual prompt.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## The Anatomy of Context {.smaller}

<style>
.dtk-ctx{--green:#0aaa50;--green-lt:#0dcc60;--green-bg:rgba(10,170,80,0.12);--green-glow:rgba(10,170,80,0.06);--card:#1a2027;--txt:#f0f6fc;--txt2:rgba(240,246,252,0.75);--txt3:rgba(240,246,252,0.55);--bdr:rgba(240,246,252,0.08);--bdr2:rgba(240,246,252,0.15);font-family:'Inter',sans-serif;padding:24px;border-radius:12px;width:100%}
.dtk-ctx .ctx-nest{display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;gap:0}
.dtk-ctx .ctx-layer{border:1.5px solid var(--bdr2);border-radius:14px;padding:18px 24px;width:100%;text-align:center;transition:border-color 0.2s}
.dtk-ctx .ctx-outer{border-color:var(--green);background:var(--green-glow)}
.dtk-ctx .ctx-children{display:flex;gap:12px;margin-top:14px}
.dtk-ctx .ctx-child{flex:1;border:1.5px solid var(--bdr2);border-radius:10px;padding:16px;background:var(--card);transition:border-color 0.2s}
.dtk-ctx .ctx-child:hover{border-color:var(--green)}
.dtk-ctx .ctx-inner{border:1.5px solid var(--green);border-radius:10px;padding:16px;margin-top:14px;background:var(--green-bg)}
.dtk-ctx .ctx-tag{font-size:10px;font-weight:600;color:var(--green);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;margin-bottom:4px}
.dtk-ctx .ctx-title{font-size:15px;font-weight:700;color:var(--txt)}
.dtk-ctx .ctx-desc{font-size:11px;color:var(--txt3);margin-top:3px}
</style>

<div class="dtk-ctx">
<div class="ctx-nest">
<div class="ctx-layer ctx-outer">
<div class="ctx-tag">The Full Context Window</div>
<div class="ctx-title">Everything the LLM Actually Sees</div>
<div class="ctx-desc">This is the real "prompt" — not just what you typed</div>
<div class="ctx-children">
<div class="ctx-child">
<div class="ctx-tag">Layer 1</div>
<div class="ctx-title">System Instructions</div>
<div class="ctx-desc">Persona, rules, constraints, output format</div>
</div>
<div class="ctx-child">
<div class="ctx-tag">Layer 2</div>
<div class="ctx-title">Tool Schemas</div>
<div class="ctx-desc">Available tools, parameters, return types</div>
</div>
<div class="ctx-child">
<div class="ctx-tag">Layer 3</div>
<div class="ctx-title">Memory & State</div>
<div class="ctx-desc">Prior conversations, session data, user preferences</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="ctx-children">
<div class="ctx-child">
<div class="ctx-tag">Layer 4</div>
<div class="ctx-title">Conversation History</div>
<div class="ctx-desc">Every prior turn in this session</div>
</div>
<div class="ctx-child">
<div class="ctx-tag">Layer 5</div>
<div class="ctx-title">Retrieved Context</div>
<div class="ctx-desc">RAG results, file contents, search results</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="ctx-inner">
<div class="ctx-tag">What you actually typed</div>
<div class="ctx-title">Your User Message</div>
<div class="ctx-desc">"Show me last quarter's revenue by region"</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Context Engineering is the #1 Skill {.center}

::: r-fit-text
[Context]{.highlight} > Prompt
:::

::: subtext
Prompt engineering is typing better questions.

Context engineering is **assembling the right information** so the model can't get it wrong.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Why context beats prompting {.incremental}

- **Prompt engineering**: "Please write a good SQL query for revenue by region"
- **Context engineering**: Give the model the schema, business glossary, sample outputs, edge cases, and constraints — *then* ask for the query
- The second approach wins every time because the model has **less room to hallucinate**

. . .

::: {.doodle-text}
You wouldn't ask a new hire to build a report without giving them access to the data dictionary. Same principle.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## The Context Window is a Container {.smaller}

<style>
.dtk-cw{--green:#0aaa50;--card:#1a2027;--txt:#f0f6fc;--txt3:rgba(240,246,252,0.55);--bdr2:rgba(240,246,252,0.15);font-family:'Inter',sans-serif;padding:20px}
.dtk-cw .cw-bar{display:flex;height:60px;border-radius:10px;overflow:hidden;border:1.5px solid var(--bdr2);margin-bottom:12px}
.dtk-cw .cw-seg{display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;font-size:11px;font-weight:600;color:var(--txt)}
.dtk-cw .cw-sys{background:var(--green);flex:2}
.dtk-cw .cw-tools{background:rgba(10,170,80,0.4);flex:1.5}
.dtk-cw .cw-mem{background:rgba(10,170,80,0.25);flex:1}
.dtk-cw .cw-conv{background:var(--card);flex:3;border-left:1px solid var(--bdr2);border-right:1px solid var(--bdr2)}
.dtk-cw .cw-rag{background:rgba(10,170,80,0.15);flex:2}
.dtk-cw .cw-user{background:rgba(10,170,80,0.08);flex:0.5}
.dtk-cw .cw-empty{background:rgba(240,246,252,0.03);flex:2;font-style:italic;color:var(--txt3)}
.dtk-cw .cw-labels{display:flex;gap:16px;justify-content:center;flex-wrap:wrap;margin-top:8px}
.dtk-cw .cw-label{font-size:10px;color:var(--txt3);display:flex;align-items:center;gap:6px}
.dtk-cw .cw-dot{width:10px;height:10px;border-radius:3px}
</style>

<div class="dtk-cw">
<div style="text-align:center;margin-bottom:16px">
<span style="font-size:14px;font-weight:700;color:var(--txt)">Context Window — 128K / 200K / 1M tokens</span><br>
<span style="font-size:11px;color:var(--txt3)">It fills up. Fast. Every token counts.</span>
</div>
<div class="cw-bar">
<div class="cw-seg cw-sys">System</div>
<div class="cw-seg cw-tools">Tools</div>
<div class="cw-seg cw-mem">Memory</div>
<div class="cw-seg cw-conv">Conversation History</div>
<div class="cw-seg cw-rag">RAG / Retrieval</div>
<div class="cw-seg cw-user">You</div>
<div class="cw-seg cw-empty">Remaining</div>
</div>
<div class="cw-labels">
<div class="cw-label"><div class="cw-dot" style="background:var(--green)"></div> System Instructions</div>
<div class="cw-label"><div class="cw-dot" style="background:rgba(10,170,80,0.4)"></div> Tool Schemas</div>
<div class="cw-label"><div class="cw-dot" style="background:rgba(10,170,80,0.25)"></div> Memory</div>
<div class="cw-label"><div class="cw-dot" style="background:var(--card);border:1px solid rgba(240,246,252,0.15)"></div> Conversation</div>
<div class="cw-label"><div class="cw-dot" style="background:rgba(10,170,80,0.15)"></div> RAG</div>
<div class="cw-label"><div class="cw-dot" style="background:rgba(10,170,80,0.08);border:1px solid rgba(240,246,252,0.1)"></div> Your Message</div>
</div>
</div>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Context Rot {.center}

::: v-center

### When conversations get long, context degrades.

Old instructions get pushed out. The model "forgets" constraints. Quality drops.

. . .

### The fix: [Progressive Compaction]{.highlight}

Summarise older turns. Prioritise recent context. Keep system instructions pinned. Refresh critical constraints.

::: {.doodle-text}
Think of it like garbage collection — but for your agent's working memory.
:::

:::


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## {.section-slide}

# Part 2

The ReAct Loop

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Think. Act. Observe. Repeat. {.smaller}

<style>
.dtk-react{--green:#0aaa50;--card:#1a2027;--txt:#f0f6fc;--txt3:rgba(240,246,252,0.55);--bdr2:rgba(240,246,252,0.15);font-family:'Inter',sans-serif;padding:20px}
.dtk-react .react-flow{display:flex;align-items:stretch;gap:0;margin:20px 0}
.dtk-react .react-step{flex:1;display:flex;align-items:center}
.dtk-react .react-box{flex:1;background:var(--card);border:1.5px solid var(--bdr2);border-radius:12px;padding:20px 16px;text-align:center;transition:border-color 0.2s}
.dtk-react .react-box:hover{border-color:var(--green)}
.dtk-react .react-box.active{border-color:var(--green);background:rgba(10,170,80,0.08)}
.dtk-react .react-num{font-size:11px;font-weight:700;color:var(--green);letter-spacing:0.05em;margin-bottom:6px}
.dtk-react .react-title{font-size:16px;font-weight:700;color:var(--txt);margin-bottom:4px}
.dtk-react .react-desc{font-size:11px;color:var(--txt3)}
.dtk-react .react-arrow{color:var(--green);font-size:18px;padding:0 8px;flex-shrink:0;opacity:0.5}
.dtk-react .react-loop{text-align:center;margin-top:12px;font-size:12px;color:var(--green);font-weight:600}
</style>

<div class="dtk-react">
<div class="react-flow">
<div class="react-step">
<div class="react-box active">
<div class="react-num">01</div>
<div class="react-title">Think</div>
<div class="react-desc">Reason about the task. Break it down. Plan the approach.</div>
</div>
<span class="react-arrow">&#9654;</span>
</div>
<div class="react-step">
<div class="react-box">
<div class="react-num">02</div>
<div class="react-title">Self-Critique</div>
<div class="react-desc">Check assumptions. Am I sure? Do I need more info first?</div>
</div>
<span class="react-arrow">&#9654;</span>
</div>
<div class="react-step">
<div class="react-box">
<div class="react-num">03</div>
<div class="react-title">Act</div>
<div class="react-desc">Call a tool. Run a query. Make a request. Do something.</div>
</div>
<span class="react-arrow">&#9654;</span>
</div>
<div class="react-step">
<div class="react-box active">
<div class="react-num">04</div>
<div class="react-title">Observe</div>
<div class="react-desc">Read the result. Did it work? What did we learn?</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="react-loop">&#8634; Loop until the task is complete or the agent decides to ask for help</div>
</div>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## The critical question at step 2 {.center .quote-slide}

> Should I **act now** with what I have? Or should I **ask a clarifying question** first?

<cite>— This is what separates a useful agent from a hallucination machine</cite>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## BI Example: The ReAct Loop in Action {.smaller}

::: icon-list
- [1]{.icon} **Think** — "The user wants revenue by region. I need the sales fact table and the region dimension."
- [2]{.icon} **Self-Critique** — "Wait — do they mean billing region or shipping region? The schema has both."
- [3]{.icon} **Act** — Ask the user: "Do you mean billing region or shipping region?"
- [4]{.icon} **Observe** — User says "billing region." Now I have what I need.
- [5]{.icon} **Act** — Execute: `SELECT region, SUM(revenue) FROM sales JOIN regions ON billing_region_id...`
- [6]{.icon} **Observe** — Results returned. 5 regions, totals look reasonable. Done.
:::

::: {.doodle-text}
The agent that asks first is the agent that gets it right.
:::


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## {.section-slide}

# Part 3

Tool Calling

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## How agents use tools {.smaller}

<style>
.dtk-tool{--green:#0aaa50;--card:#1a2027;--txt:#f0f6fc;--txt3:rgba(240,246,252,0.55);--bdr2:rgba(240,246,252,0.15);font-family:'Inter',sans-serif;padding:20px}
.dtk-tool .tool-flow{display:flex;align-items:stretch;gap:0;margin:16px 0}
.dtk-tool .tool-step{flex:1;display:flex;align-items:center}
.dtk-tool .tool-box{flex:1;background:var(--card);border:1.5px solid var(--bdr2);border-radius:12px;padding:18px 14px;text-align:center;transition:border-color 0.2s}
.dtk-tool .tool-box:hover{border-color:var(--green)}
.dtk-tool .tool-num{font-size:10px;font-weight:700;color:var(--green);letter-spacing:0.05em;margin-bottom:4px}
.dtk-tool .tool-title{font-size:15px;font-weight:700;color:var(--txt);margin-bottom:3px}
.dtk-tool .tool-desc{font-size:10px;color:var(--txt3)}
.dtk-tool .tool-arrow{color:var(--green);font-size:16px;padding:0 6px;flex-shrink:0;opacity:0.5}
</style>

<div class="dtk-tool">
<div class="tool-flow">
<div class="tool-step">
<div class="tool-box">
<div class="tool-num">01</div>
<div class="tool-title">Discover</div>
<div class="tool-desc">Read available tool schemas from the context</div>
</div>
<span class="tool-arrow">&#9654;</span>
</div>
<div class="tool-step">
<div class="tool-box">
<div class="tool-num">02</div>
<div class="tool-title">Select</div>
<div class="tool-desc">Pick the right tool for this step of the task</div>
</div>
<span class="tool-arrow">&#9654;</span>
</div>
<div class="tool-step">
<div class="tool-box">
<div class="tool-num">03</div>
<div class="tool-title">Parameterise</div>
<div class="tool-desc">Fill in the required arguments from context</div>
</div>
<span class="tool-arrow">&#9654;</span>
</div>
<div class="tool-step">
<div class="tool-box">
<div class="tool-num">04</div>
<div class="tool-title">Invoke</div>
<div class="tool-desc">Execute the tool call and wait for results</div>
</div>
<span class="tool-arrow">&#9654;</span>
</div>
<div class="tool-step">
<div class="tool-box">
<div class="tool-num">05</div>
<div class="tool-title">Validate</div>
<div class="tool-desc">Check the result — retry or move on</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## A tool schema is a contract {.smaller}

The LLM doesn't "know" what your tools do. It reads the schema and infers behaviour.

``` json
{
  "name": "query_semantic_model",
  "description": "Execute a DAX query against the semantic model",
  "parameters": {
    "model_name": {
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Name of the Power BI semantic model"
    },
    "dax_query": {
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Valid DAX query to execute"
    },
    "max_rows": {
      "type": "integer",
      "description": "Maximum rows to return (default 1000)"
    }
  }
}
```

::: {.doodle-text}
Better descriptions = better tool selection = better results. This is context engineering again.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## BI-Specific Tools {.center}

::: card-grid
::: card
### Query Engine
Execute DAX / SQL against semantic models and warehouses
:::

::: card
### Schema Explorer
Browse tables, columns, relationships, and measures
:::

::: card
### Dashboard API
Read visuals, get filter states, trigger refreshes
:::

::: card
### Data Profiler
Sample data, check distributions, spot anomalies
:::
:::

::: subtext
The tools you give an agent define what it *can* do. Choose carefully.
:::


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## {.section-slide}

# Part 4

Agent Skills

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Skills = Tools + Domain Instructions {.center}

::: icons-row
::: {.icon-circle .green}
&#128295;
:::

[+]{.plus}

::: icon-circle
&#128218;
:::

[=]{.plus}

::: {.icon-circle .green}
&#127919;
:::
:::

::: subtext
Tools (what it can do) + Domain Instructions (how to do it well) = A Skill
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## BI Skills in Practice {.smaller}

::: card-grid
::: card
### Data Quality Skill

**Tools**: profiler, schema explorer, query engine

**Instructions**: "Check for nulls in key columns. Flag cardinality mismatches. Compare row counts against yesterday. Report anomalies, not just stats."
:::

::: card
### Report Builder Skill

**Tools**: query engine, dashboard API, chart renderer

**Instructions**: "Always start with a summary metric. Use the corporate colour palette. Add data labels. Never use pie charts with more than 5 slices."
:::

::: card
### Metric Explorer Skill

**Tools**: semantic model, glossary lookup, query engine

**Instructions**: "Always resolve metric names against the business glossary first. Show the DAX definition before executing. Confirm the time grain."
:::

::: card
### Incident Responder Skill

**Tools**: query engine, refresh API, alert system

**Instructions**: "Check refresh status first. If stale, check source connectivity. Escalate if more than 2 hours behind SLA."
:::
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Skills shape capability {.center}

::: v-center

An agent without skills is a chatbot with tools.

An agent [with]{.highlight} skills knows **when** to use each tool, **how** to combine them, and **what good looks like**.

::: {.doodle-text}
Skills are where your domain expertise lives.
:::

:::


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## {.section-slide}

# Part 5

Hooks & Security

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## The problem with letting agents run free {.center .quote-slide}

> An agent with a SQL tool and no guardrails is a `DROP TABLE` waiting to happen.

<cite>— The uncomfortable truth about agent security</cite>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Pre-call and Post-call Hooks {.smaller}

<style>
.dtk-hook{--green:#0aaa50;--card:#1a2027;--txt:#f0f6fc;--txt3:rgba(240,246,252,0.55);--bdr2:rgba(240,246,252,0.15);--red:#ef4444;--orange:#f59e0b;font-family:'Inter',sans-serif;padding:20px}
.dtk-hook .hook-flow{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:0;margin:16px 0}
.dtk-hook .hook-box{background:var(--card);border:1.5px solid var(--bdr2);border-radius:12px;padding:20px 18px;text-align:center;flex:1;transition:border-color 0.2s}
.dtk-hook .hook-box:hover{border-color:var(--green)}
.dtk-hook .hook-box.pre{border-color:var(--orange);background:rgba(245,158,11,0.06)}
.dtk-hook .hook-box.post{border-color:var(--green);background:rgba(10,170,80,0.06)}
.dtk-hook .hook-box.core{border-color:var(--bdr2);flex:1.5}
.dtk-hook .hook-tag{font-size:10px;font-weight:700;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;margin-bottom:4px}
.dtk-hook .hook-tag.pre{color:var(--orange)}
.dtk-hook .hook-tag.post{color:var(--green)}
.dtk-hook .hook-tag.core{color:var(--txt3)}
.dtk-hook .hook-title{font-size:15px;font-weight:700;color:var(--txt);margin-bottom:4px}
.dtk-hook .hook-desc{font-size:10px;color:var(--txt3)}
.dtk-hook .hook-arrow{color:var(--green);font-size:18px;padding:0 10px;flex-shrink:0;opacity:0.5}
</style>

<div class="dtk-hook">
<div class="hook-flow">
<div class="hook-box pre">
<div class="hook-tag pre">Pre-Call Hook</div>
<div class="hook-title">Intercept & Validate</div>
<div class="hook-desc">Block PII in queries. Validate parameters. Enforce read-only. Check permissions.</div>
</div>
<span class="hook-arrow">&#9654;</span>
<div class="hook-box core">
<div class="hook-tag core">Tool Execution</div>
<div class="hook-title">Agent Calls Tool</div>
<div class="hook-desc">query_semantic_model("SELECT customer_name, revenue FROM sales")</div>
</div>
<span class="hook-arrow">&#9654;</span>
<div class="hook-box post">
<div class="hook-tag post">Post-Call Hook</div>
<div class="hook-title">Sanitise & Log</div>
<div class="hook-desc">Mask sensitive fields. Validate output shape. Log for audit trail.</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Real-world hook patterns {.smaller}

::: icon-list
- [&#128683;]{.icon} **PII Blocking** — Pre-hook scans DAX/SQL for customer names, emails, or personal identifiers and blocks execution
- [&#128274;]{.icon} **Read-Only Enforcement** — Pre-hook rejects any query containing INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or DROP
- [&#128101;]{.icon} **Row-Level Security** — Pre-hook injects WHERE clauses based on the user's security context
- [&#128203;]{.icon} **Audit Logging** — Post-hook records every tool call, parameters, results, and who triggered it
- [&#9888;]{.icon} **Output Sanitisation** — Post-hook masks credit card numbers, national IDs, and other sensitive data in results
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Without hooks, you're hoping for the best {.center}

::: v-center

Hooks are where [policy becomes enforcement]{.doodle-circle .highlight}.

They run outside the model. The LLM can't override them. That's the point.

:::


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## {.section-slide}

# Part 6

Agent Harnesses

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What's a harness? {.smaller}

<style>
.dtk-harness{--green:#0aaa50;--green-bg:rgba(10,170,80,0.12);--green-glow:rgba(10,170,80,0.06);--card:#1a2027;--txt:#f0f6fc;--txt3:rgba(240,246,252,0.55);--bdr:rgba(240,246,252,0.08);--bdr2:rgba(240,246,252,0.15);font-family:'Inter',sans-serif;padding:20px}
.dtk-harness .h-nest{display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;gap:0}
.dtk-harness .h-layer{border:1.5px solid var(--bdr2);border-radius:14px;padding:18px 24px;width:100%;text-align:center;transition:border-color 0.2s}
.dtk-harness .h-outer{border-color:var(--green);background:var(--green-glow)}
.dtk-harness .h-children{display:flex;gap:12px;margin-top:14px}
.dtk-harness .h-child{flex:1;border:1.5px solid var(--bdr2);border-radius:10px;padding:16px;background:var(--card);transition:border-color 0.2s}
.dtk-harness .h-child:hover{border-color:var(--green)}
.dtk-harness .h-inner{border:1.5px solid var(--green);border-radius:10px;padding:16px;margin-top:14px;background:var(--green-bg)}
.dtk-harness .h-tag{font-size:10px;font-weight:600;color:var(--green);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;margin-bottom:4px}
.dtk-harness .h-title{font-size:15px;font-weight:700;color:var(--txt)}
.dtk-harness .h-desc{font-size:10px;color:var(--txt3);margin-top:3px}
.dtk-harness .h-bars{display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px;width:100%;margin-top:16px}
.dtk-harness .h-bar{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:10px;padding:8px 14px;background:var(--card);border:1px solid var(--bdr);border-radius:8px;transition:border-color 0.2s}
.dtk-harness .h-bar:hover{border-color:var(--green)}
.dtk-harness .h-bar-icon{font-size:12px;color:var(--green)}
.dtk-harness .h-bar-text{font-size:11px;font-weight:500;color:rgba(240,246,252,0.75)}
</style>

<div class="dtk-harness">
<div class="h-nest">
<div class="h-layer h-outer">
<div class="h-tag">The Agent Harness</div>
<div class="h-title">Orchestration & Safety Layer</div>
<div class="h-desc">Everything that wraps around the LLM to make it usable</div>
<div class="h-children">
<div class="h-child">
<div class="h-tag">Context</div>
<div class="h-title">Message History</div>
<div class="h-desc">Manages conversation, compaction, token budgets</div>
</div>
<div class="h-child">
<div class="h-tag">Execution</div>
<div class="h-title">Tool Runtime</div>
<div class="h-desc">Dispatches tool calls, handles errors, retries</div>
</div>
<div class="h-child">
<div class="h-tag">Safety</div>
<div class="h-title">Guardrails</div>
<div class="h-desc">Approval gates, iteration caps, doom loop detection</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="h-inner">
<div class="h-tag">The Core</div>
<div class="h-title">LLM (Claude / GPT / etc.)</div>
<div class="h-desc">The model itself — just one piece of the puzzle</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="h-bars">
<div class="h-bar">
<span class="h-bar-icon">&#9670;</span>
<span class="h-bar-text">Hooks Layer — pre/post call interception</span>
</div>
<div class="h-bar">
<span class="h-bar-icon">&#9670;</span>
<span class="h-bar-text">State & Memory — session persistence, user preferences</span>
</div>
<div class="h-bar">
<span class="h-bar-icon">&#9670;</span>
<span class="h-bar-text">Observability — logging, tracing, cost tracking</span>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Safety layers that matter {.incremental}

- **Approval gates** — High-risk actions require human confirmation before execution
- **Iteration caps** — Stop after N tool calls to prevent runaway loops
- **Doom loop detection** — If the agent repeats the same failing action, break the cycle
- **Cost guardrails** — Token budgets and spend limits per session
- **Sandboxing** — Execute code in isolated environments, not on production

. . .

::: {.doodle-text}
The harness is why you can sleep at night.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Harnesses simplify as models improve {.center}

::: comparison
::: box
### 2024 Harness

Heavy orchestration. Complex state machines. Multi-step planners. Lots of code wrapping the LLM.
:::

[→]{.arrow}

::: {.box .active}
### 2026 Harness

Thinner. The model handles more reasoning natively. The harness focuses on safety, tools, and context — not control flow.
:::
:::

::: {.subtext .doodle-text}
The Manus / Claude Code lesson: the best harness is the one the model doesn't fight against.
:::


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## {.section-slide}

# Part 7

The Agent Maturity Model

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Where is your organisation? {.smaller}

<style>
.dtk-maturity{--green:#0aaa50;--green-lt:#0dcc60;--green-bg:rgba(10,170,80,0.12);--green-glow:rgba(10,170,80,0.06);--card:#1a2027;--txt:#f0f6fc;--txt3:rgba(240,246,252,0.55);--bdr:rgba(240,246,252,0.08);--bdr2:rgba(240,246,252,0.15);font-family:'Inter',sans-serif;padding:20px}
.dtk-maturity .mat-stairs{display:flex;align-items:flex-end;gap:0;min-height:320px;margin:16px 0}
.dtk-maturity .mat-step{display:flex;flex-direction:column;justify-content:center;padding:20px 24px;border:1.5px solid var(--bdr2);border-radius:12px 12px 0 0;text-align:left;transition:border-color 0.2s}
.dtk-maturity .mat-step:hover{border-color:var(--green)}
.dtk-maturity .mat-s1{flex:4;min-height:90px;background:var(--green-bg);border-color:var(--green)}
.dtk-maturity .mat-s2{flex:3;min-height:190px;background:var(--card)}
.dtk-maturity .mat-s3{flex:2.5;min-height:300px;background:var(--green-glow);border-color:var(--green)}
.dtk-maturity .mat-num{font-size:10px;font-weight:700;color:var(--green);margin-bottom:4px}
.dtk-maturity .mat-title{font-size:17px;font-weight:700;color:var(--txt);margin-bottom:4px}
.dtk-maturity .mat-desc{font-size:11px;color:var(--txt3);line-height:1.4}
.dtk-maturity .mat-arrow{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;margin-top:16px;justify-content:center}
.dtk-maturity .mat-arrow span{font-size:11px;font-weight:500;color:var(--txt3)}
.dtk-maturity .mat-arrow-line{flex:1;height:1px;background:linear-gradient(to right,transparent,var(--green))}
</style>

<div class="dtk-maturity">
<div class="mat-stairs">
<div class="mat-step mat-s1">
<div class="mat-num">Level 1 — Foundation</div>
<div class="mat-title">Single Agent</div>
<div class="mat-desc">One agent, one task, deterministic flow. Copilots. Chat-with-your-data.</div>
</div>
<div class="mat-step mat-s2">
<div class="mat-num">Level 2 — Orchestration</div>
<div class="mat-title">Sub-Agents</div>
<div class="mat-desc">Specialised agents for decomposed tasks. Supervisor patterns. A "planner" delegates to "workers."</div>
</div>
<div class="mat-step mat-s3">
<div class="mat-num">Level 3 — Autonomy</div>
<div class="mat-title">Agent Swarms</div>
<div class="mat-desc">Agent-to-agent communication. Self-directed task allocation. Collaborative capability building.</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="mat-arrow">
<span>Increasing autonomy, complexity, and value</span>
<div class="mat-arrow-line"></div>
<span style="color:var(--green);">&#9650;</span>
</div>
</div>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Level 1 — Single Agent Workflows {.smaller}

::: columns
::: column
### What it looks like

- One agent handles one task end-to-end
- Deterministic flow — you know what it'll do
- "Chat with your data" assistants
- Copilot experiences in Power BI, Excel

### Who's here

Most organisations. And that's [OK]{.highlight}.
:::

::: column
### BI Examples

::: icon-list
- [&#128202;]{.icon} **Report Q&A** — Ask questions about a specific dashboard
- [&#128269;]{.icon} **Data Profiling** — "Check this table for quality issues"
- [&#9997;]{.icon} **SQL Generation** — Natural language to query
- [&#128200;]{.icon} **Anomaly Alerts** — "Flag anything unusual in yesterday's load"
:::
:::
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Level 2 — Sub-Agent Orchestration {.smaller}

::: columns
::: column
### What it looks like

- A **supervisor agent** decomposes the task
- **Specialist agents** handle sub-tasks
- Results flow back to the supervisor for synthesis
- More capable, but more complex to manage
:::

::: column
### BI Example

::: icon-list
- [&#127919;]{.icon} **Supervisor** — "Analyse Q1 performance across all regions"
- [&#128202;]{.icon} **Agent A** — Queries revenue data
- [&#128200;]{.icon} **Agent B** — Runs trend analysis
- [&#128203;]{.icon} **Agent C** — Checks data quality
- [&#127919;]{.icon} **Supervisor** — Synthesises findings into executive summary
:::
:::
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Level 3 — Autonomous Agents & Swarms {.smaller}

::: columns
::: column
### What it looks like

- Agents communicate with each other directly
- Self-directed task allocation
- Agents can spawn new agents as needed
- Collaborative capability building

### The honest truth

Very few orgs are here. And most don't need to be — yet.
:::

::: column
### The Future BI Vision

::: icon-list
- [&#129302;]{.icon} **Monitoring swarm** — Agents watching every pipeline, model, and dashboard
- [&#128161;]{.icon} **Insight swarm** — Agents proactively surfacing business-relevant anomalies
- [&#128736;]{.icon} **Self-healing data** — Agents detecting and fixing quality issues automatically
:::
:::
:::


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Assess where you are {.center}

<style>
.dtk-assess{--green:#0aaa50;--card:#1a2027;--txt:#f0f6fc;--txt3:rgba(240,246,252,0.55);--bdr:rgba(240,246,252,0.08);--bdr2:rgba(240,246,252,0.15);font-family:'Inter',sans-serif;padding:20px}
.dtk-assess .assess-grid{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:12px;margin:16px 0}
.dtk-assess .assess-cell{background:var(--card);border:1.5px solid var(--bdr2);border-radius:12px;padding:18px;text-align:left}
.dtk-assess .assess-cell.yes{border-color:var(--green);background:rgba(10,170,80,0.06)}
.dtk-assess .assess-tag{font-size:10px;font-weight:700;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.06em;margin-bottom:6px}
.dtk-assess .assess-tag.g{color:var(--green)}
.dtk-assess .assess-tag.w{color:var(--txt3)}
.dtk-assess .assess-q{font-size:13px;font-weight:600;color:var(--txt);line-height:1.3}
</style>

<div class="dtk-assess">
<div class="assess-grid">
<div class="assess-cell yes">
<div class="assess-tag g">Level 1 &#10003;</div>
<div class="assess-q">Do your agents have well-defined context with system instructions and tool schemas?</div>
</div>
<div class="assess-cell yes">
<div class="assess-tag g">Level 1 &#10003;</div>
<div class="assess-q">Do you have hooks for security and audit logging on tool calls?</div>
</div>
<div class="assess-cell">
<div class="assess-tag w">Level 2</div>
<div class="assess-q">Can your agents decompose tasks and delegate to specialised sub-agents?</div>
</div>
<div class="assess-cell">
<div class="assess-tag w">Level 2</div>
<div class="assess-q">Do you have a supervisor pattern with result synthesis?</div>
</div>
<div class="assess-cell">
<div class="assess-tag w">Level 3</div>
<div class="assess-q">Can agents communicate directly with other agents without human routing?</div>
</div>
<div class="assess-cell">
<div class="assess-tag w">Level 3</div>
<div class="assess-q">Do agents self-direct and spawn new agents based on task needs?</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

::: {.doodle-text}
Most teams should master Level 1 before chasing Level 3. Foundations first.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## {.section-slide}

# Bringing It All Together

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## The full picture {.smaller}

<style>
.dtk-full{--green:#0aaa50;--card:#1a2027;--txt:#f0f6fc;--txt3:rgba(240,246,252,0.55);--bdr2:rgba(240,246,252,0.15);font-family:'Inter',sans-serif;padding:16px}
.dtk-full .full-flow{display:flex;align-items:stretch;gap:0;margin:12px 0}
.dtk-full .full-step{flex:1;display:flex;align-items:center}
.dtk-full .full-box{flex:1;background:var(--card);border:1.5px solid var(--bdr2);border-radius:10px;padding:14px 10px;text-align:center;transition:border-color 0.2s}
.dtk-full .full-box:hover{border-color:var(--green)}
.dtk-full .full-box.hl{border-color:var(--green);background:rgba(10,170,80,0.06)}
.dtk-full .full-num{font-size:9px;font-weight:700;color:var(--green);letter-spacing:0.05em;margin-bottom:3px}
.dtk-full .full-title{font-size:13px;font-weight:700;color:var(--txt);margin-bottom:2px}
.dtk-full .full-desc{font-size:9px;color:var(--txt3)}
.dtk-full .full-arrow{color:var(--green);font-size:14px;padding:0 5px;flex-shrink:0;opacity:0.5}
</style>

<div class="dtk-full">
<div class="full-flow">
<div class="full-step">
<div class="full-box hl">
<div class="full-num">01</div>
<div class="full-title">Context</div>
<div class="full-desc">Assemble the right information</div>
</div>
<span class="full-arrow">&#9654;</span>
</div>
<div class="full-step">
<div class="full-box">
<div class="full-num">02</div>
<div class="full-title">ReAct</div>
<div class="full-desc">Think, critique, act, observe</div>
</div>
<span class="full-arrow">&#9654;</span>
</div>
<div class="full-step">
<div class="full-box">
<div class="full-num">03</div>
<div class="full-title">Tools</div>
<div class="full-desc">Discover, select, invoke</div>
</div>
<span class="full-arrow">&#9654;</span>
</div>
<div class="full-step">
<div class="full-box">
<div class="full-num">04</div>
<div class="full-title">Skills</div>
<div class="full-desc">Domain knowledge + tools</div>
</div>
<span class="full-arrow">&#9654;</span>
</div>
<div class="full-step">
<div class="full-box">
<div class="full-num">05</div>
<div class="full-title">Hooks</div>
<div class="full-desc">Policy enforcement</div>
</div>
<span class="full-arrow">&#9654;</span>
</div>
<div class="full-step">
<div class="full-box hl">
<div class="full-num">06</div>
<div class="full-title">Harness</div>
<div class="full-desc">Safety & orchestration</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

::: subtext
These six concepts are the building blocks of every agent system. Master them, and you can build — or evaluate — anything.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Key takeaways {.incremental}

- [Context engineering]{.highlight} is the #1 skill — assemble the right information and the model can't get it wrong
- The [ReAct loop]{.highlight} is how agents think — and knowing when to ask vs act is what makes them useful
- [Tools]{.highlight} define capability. [Skills]{.highlight} define competence. Get both right.
- [Hooks]{.highlight} are where security policy becomes enforcement — outside the model's control
- The [harness]{.highlight} keeps everything safe. Start simple and let the model do more over time.
- [Level 1 is not a consolation prize]{.highlight} — it's the foundation everything else depends on

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## One more thing. {.center}

::: v-center

::: r-fit-text
Start with [Level 1]{.doodle-circle .highlight}.
:::

Get context engineering right. Add tools. Add hooks. Build trust.

The maturity will follow.

:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Thank You! {.section-slide}

### Questions?

[myyearindata.com]{.highlight}

@fusionet24
